page: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography, though it lacks x86-specific vector extensions such as AVX or AMX. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The c8g.large achieves strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression tasks, with gzip compression reaching 30.27 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.15 GB/sec. Single-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, register average performance levels. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations, including bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth is mixed, showing average performance for large-block read-only operations at 55.26 GB/sec, but weak performance for write operations and small-block cached reads. Database operations under Passmark score in the average range, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput are relatively weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8g.large offers a streamlined, entry-level compute-optimized profile. The dedicated core allocation and high single-threaded efficiency make it a viable option for workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or large memory footprints. It is well-suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, minor database operations, and simple web serving tasks. However, the limited 4.0 GB memory capacity and weak multi-core scaling make it less suitable for intensive software compilation, complex image processing, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor on the arm64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of system memory, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, particularly in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside average single-core CPU and database performance. However, multi-core scaling, memory write bandwidth, and software compilation benchmarks are weak. Qualitatively, the c8g.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for dedicated compute resources. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and modest database operations, while being constrained for memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.large is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance delivering strong single-threaded performance and dedicated Graviton cores for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 36 MB L3 cache
Strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications and basic database tasks
